scheme_name: Mukhya Mantri Sarvajan Uchcha Shiksha Chhatravriti Yojana
page_title: Mukhya Mantri Sarvajan Uchcha Shiksha Chhatravriti Yojana

details: The scheme "Mukhyamantri Sarvajan Uchcha Shiksha Chhatravriti Yojana" was launched by the Department of Social Justice & Empowerment, Government of Rajasthan. The main objective of this scheme is to provide financial assistance to students of all sections & castes of Rajasthan who are pursuing higher education in nationally recognized institutions. Under this scheme, 50% of the non-refundable institutional fees are reimbursed to eligible students studying in IITs, IIMs, and other premier institutions.
benefits: Students receive 50% reimbursement of their non-refundable institutional fees (such as tuition, library, medical, and examination fees).
The scholarship amount is transferred directly to the student's bank account.
The scholarship is disbursed annually throughout the duration of the course.
Includes IITs, IIMs, NITs, AIIMS, and other national institutions listed in the
eligibility: The applicant should be a native of Rajasthan.
The annual income of the parent/guardian of the student/applicant from all sources (including the applicant’s own income, if any) should be less than ₹5,00,000/-.
The applicant should be enrolled in IITs, IIMs, NITs, AIIMS, or other
The applicant should not be availing of any other government scholarship for the same course.
The applicant should maintain academic performance as required for renewal of the scholarship.
An employee pursuing full-time studies while on leave without pay for the entire duration of the course will also be eligible for the scholarship.

online: Then you will be redirected to the SSO registration page. The registration page will appear with the following options.
Choose the either one option from the Jan Aadhaar Or Google to process further.
Jan Aadhaar : Enter the Jan Aadhaar number, click on the ‘Next’ button,
Select your name, the name of the head of the family and all the other members and
Click on the ‘Send OTP’ button. Enter the ‘OTP’ and
Click on the ‘Verify OTP’ button to Complete the registration.
Google : Enter the Gmail ID, click on the ‘Next’ button,
A new link appear on screen, now click on new SSO link.
SSO id will appear on screen, now create the password.
Enter Mobile number, click on registration.
Now click on "Apply for Scheme" on screen.
In this new page, will get a list of names of all the schemes, and click on the name of "SCHOLARSHIP (SJE)"

documents_required: Death Certificate of parent/spouse, if applicable
Admission Letter from the Recognized Institution
Fee Receipt from the Institution (showing non-refundable components)
Caste Certificate (if applicable for reservation benefits)
Employer Certificate (for government employees on study leave)

faqs.question: Who is eligible for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Students who are domiciles of Rajasthan, studying in nationally recognized institutions, and have an annual family income of ₹5,00,000 or less are eligible.

faqs.question: Can students studying outside Rajasthan apply?
faqs.answer: Yes, if they are domiciles of Rajasthan and studying in an institution listed under the scheme guidelines.

faqs.question: What expenses are covered under non-refundable fees?
faqs.answer: Non-refundable fees include admission, tuition, registration, medical, library, and examination fees.

faqs.question: How can I apply for the scholarship?
faqs.answer: Students must apply online through the official Rajasthan government scholarship portal.

faqs.question: Is the scholarship renewed every year?
faqs.answer: Yes, students must submit a renewal application every year with their academic performance details.

faqs.question: What happens if I fail an exam?
faqs.answer: If the student is promoted despite failing, they remain eligible for the scholarship.

faqs.question: What is the last date to apply for the scholarship?
faqs.answer: The last date varies each year and is announced on the official website.

faqs.question: Can married female students apply?
faqs.answer: Yes, but only the husband’s income is considered for eligibility.

faqs.question: What if my parents are retired or deceased?
faqs.answer: A pension statement or guardian’s income proof must be submitted.

faqs.question: Is there an age limit to apply for the scholarship?
faqs.answer: No specific age limit is mentioned in the scheme.

faqs.question: Can students from private universities apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, only students studying in government-recognized premier institutions like IITs, IIMs, NITs, AIIMS, etc. are eligible under this scheme.
